subject: DevOps
title: Service Mesh Concepts
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Service Mesh Concepts.

hook.text: Fifty microservices call each other. How do you encrypt every link and trace every request?

buildup.text: A service mesh adds a sidecar proxy beside each service. All traffic flows through the proxy.

discovery.text: Istio and Linkerd provide mutual TLS, retries, traffic shaping, and observability out of the box.

twist.text: Sidecars add latency and resource cost. For small setups, a mesh is overkill. It shines at scale.

climax.text: Canary deploys get easy: route 5% to the new version, watch metrics, then ramp up gradually.

punchline.text: A service mesh handles the hard parts of service networking.

quiz.question: What does a sidecar proxy do in a service mesh?
options: Handles networking concerns like encryption, retries, and observability for the service
Replaces the service entirely with a proxy version
Only logs error messages from the service
